commands.review_summary:
The branch changes JSON-RPC `send` so reply-target aliases are forwarded to the bridge as `selectedMessageGuid`, blocks AppleScript fallback for threaded reply sends, and adds focused bridge tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main never reads `reply_to` on plain RPC `send` and never forwards `selectedMessageGuid` to the bridge. I did not run a live macOS Messages/chat.db reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor-provided live proof plus maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed reply fallback behavior, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: fix: thread rpc send replies
target.body:
Closes #144.

Plain RPC `send` now forwards reply target aliases into the bridge `selectedMessageGuid` payload, matching `send.rich` behavior.

Reply-target sends now fail instead of falling back to unthreaded AppleScript delivery when the bridge is unavailable or fails.

Proof note: on a macOS arm64 branch-built `imsg` with SIP enabled and no bridge helper (`bridge_version: 0`), RPC `send` with `reply_to` returns `Invalid params: reply_to requires bridge transport; AppleScript fallback cannot send threaded replies`, so the no-bridge path fails before AppleScript delivery. Positive threaded-send proof has been requested from Mantis because this local machine cannot inject the bridge.

Verified with focused RPC bridge coverage plus the full test and lint gates.

commands.author: TurboTheTurtle
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an offline-validation provider recommendation use case, credentialless aliases, provider scoring, docs, a changelog entry, and focused CLI tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a new provider recommendation use case rather than a broken existing behavior report. Source inspection confirmed current main does not already provide the requested use case.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The only concrete automated repair is removing the release-owned CHANGELOG.md edit; product acceptance of the new recommendation remains maintainer-owned.

target.title: feat(providers): recommend offline validation
target.body:
Summary:
- add an offline-validation provider recommendation use case and credentialless aliases
- rank local runtimes/sandboxes/VMs first, with BYO SSH and external-provider fallbacks
- document the workflow and changelog entry

Verification:
- go test -count=1 ./internal/cli -run 'TestProvidersRecommend(OfflineValidation|NoCredentials|ListsUseCases)'
- go run ./cmd/crabbox providers recommend offline-validation --json
- go run ./cmd/crabbox providers recommend no-credentials --limit 1 --json
- git diff --check
- go test -count=1 ./internal/cli
- go test -race -count=1 ./internal/cli -run 'TestProvidersRecommend(OfflineValidation|NoCredentials)'
target.author: vincentkoc
